data.title: From Wildfires to Firewalls: BlackRock et al Leave Climate Coalitions
data.description:
As wildfires blaze in California and a fierce storm brews in North Carolina, Wall Street’s climate pledges go up in smoke as BlackRock and others back out of climate conventions before Trump’s inauguration.

California Wildfires Highlight Increasing Climate Disasters

Los Angeles is in flames—again. Wildfires have claimed at least ten lives, scorched thousands of homes and businesses, and wreaked havoc in California neighborhoods like upscale Pacific Palisades.

Reuters
@Reuters

The most destructive wildfires ever for Los Angeles may cause billions of dollars in insured losses, ratings agencies said, though many homes are likely uninsured https://t.co/DjxTgwGtYO pic.twitter.com/lSFIddauTN

Jan 10, 2025

Famous names like Jamie Lee Curtis and Billy Crystal are among the victims who lost their homes in the inferno. Officials traced the fire’s origins to a wooded arroyo behind a house on Piedra Morada Drive, but lightning—often the usual suspect—was ruled out early. The fires, driven by relentless winds, underscore California’s ongoing battle with climate-fueled catastrophes.

North Carolina: Storm Clouds and Stark Realities

While California fights fires, North Carolina braces for a storm the likes of which of the state hasn’t seen in decades. North Carolina is preparing for a massive storm predicted to bring serious amounts of snow and ice, enough to cut power in the middle of the winter. As the storm looms, residents are being urged to prepare to as Duke Energy, the local power provider, warns that half an inch of ice could well damage power lines.

Climate extremes are becoming the norm, and the stark contrast between the fire-scorched West and the storm-battered East couldn’t be clearer. But as nature rages on both coasts, Wall Street has quietly retreated from its climate commitments.

Wall Street’s Climate Backpedal: Banks Bail on Net-Zero

In a move as subtle as a fire alarm in a windstorm, major U.S. institutions—including BlackRock—have withdrawn from net-zero alliances just days before Trump’s inauguration. The Net Zero Banking Alliance, a UN-backed coalition that commits financial institutions to align lending and investment portfolios with net-zero emissions by 2050, has lost some of its biggest players. JP Morgan has become the latest major bank to exit the UN-backed Alliance, joining Citigroup, Bank of America, Morgan Stanley, Wells Fargo, and Goldman Sachs. All six institutions have withdrawn since early December.

Prof Michael E. Mann
@MichaelEMann

I discussed the role that human-caused climate change is playing in the devastating Southern California wildfires with @BriKeilarcnn on @CNN earlier today: pic.twitter.com/aTRGsZdDMD

Jan 08, 2025

BlackRock, meanwhile, have announced that they intend to leave the Net Zero Asset Managers Initiative. The Initiative is a global coalition of asset managers dedicated to achieving net-zero greenhouse gas emissions by 2050 or earlier. This commitment aligns with their fiduciary responsibilities to clients and beneficiaries and supports global efforts to limit temperature increases to 1.5 degrees Celsius. The initiative also promotes investment strategies consistent with the goal of reaching net-zero emissions within the same timeframe.

BlackRock, the world’s largest asset manager, cited mounting political and legal pressures as reasons for its exit. ESG (Environmental, Social, and Governance) investing, once a golden ticket for Wall Street, has become a lightning rod for criticism from conservative lawmakers and regulators.

The timing? Impeccable. The decision? Controversial. The message? Climate commitments are optional when profits are on the line.

California, Climate, and Cash: A Toxic Triangle

The irony is rich—almost as rich as the banks themselves. While California burns, North Carolina braces for impact, Wall Street’s retreat from climate pledges sends a clear signal: climate change is bad for business.

Reuters
@Reuters

Damage and economic loss from the California wildfires, one of the worst in history, could exceed $50 billion, AccuWeather estimated https://t.co/6BMs4CiH0H pic.twitter.com/yeIjUHWLgr

Jan 09, 2025

California’s wildfires are already wreaking havoc on insurers, utility companies, and real estate markets. Homeowners in fire-prone areas are seeing skyrocketing insurance premiums or losing coverage altogether. The fires are not just environmental disasters; they’re economic ones, too.

BlackRock’s decision to bail on net-zero comes as it faces shareholder scrutiny and political pushback over its ESG practices. For all the talk of green energy and climate responsibility, the financial sector’s actions speak louder than its marketing slogans.

Storms Ahead: What’s Next for Climate Finance?

With Wall Street retreating and natural disasters escalating, the future of climate finance is murky at best. BlackRock and other banks argue that they’re still committed to sustainability, just not under the constraints of alliances like Net Zero. Critics, however, see this as a clear abdication of responsibility.

Flames, Floods, and Financial Follies

California’s wildfires and North Carolina’s storms are not just weather events—they’re wake-up calls. Yet Wall Street seems to have hit the snooze button. As BlackRock and its peers step back from climate commitments, the disconnect between environmental urgency and financial priorities has never been starker.

For now, the fires rage, the storm looms, and Wall Street watches from the sidelines, calculating the cost of doing nothing.

For more stories around the edge of finance, follow our Trending section.

data.title: Hong Kong Shares Fall 0.9%; Eyes on U.S. Data — Market Talk
data.description: Hong Kong's Hang Seng Index closed 0.9% lower at 19064.29, erasing earlier gains. Investors are waiting for U.S. nonfarm payrolls data due later, which could influence the Federal Reserve's rate cut trajectory. Expectations around the Fed's rate path affect appetite for riskier assets like stocks. Chinese property developer Sunac China slumped 26% after the company received a winding up petition. Other decliners include JD Health International, which dropped 5.1%, and Lenovo Group, which lost 4.9%. Meanwhile, Country Garden Services rose 5.2% and Zijin Mining Group added 3.1%. (amanda.lee@wsj.com)

data.title: Hang Seng Sinks 3.5% This Week
data.description:
The Hang Seng fell 177 points or 0.9% to close at 19,064 on Friday, declining for the fifth session and reaching its lowest in over six weeks due to broad-based sector losses.

Nervous traders reacted to the PBoC’s decision to pause treasury bond purchases which triggered a rise in yields.

Investors cautiously awaited China’s December trade data, due over the weekend.

In November, exports grew less than expected, while imports unexpectedly shrank.

Meanwhile, US futures pointed to a weaker open on Wall Street, with traders bracing for NFP data later today.

For the week, the index slumped 3.4%, marking its second straight weekly drop amid mounting concerns that Chinese stocks were nearing bear-market territory.

Additionally, the latest FOMC meeting minutes suggested persistent inflation risks, potentially capping the scope for rate cuts this year.

Among single stocks, steep losses included JD Health Intl. (-4.8%), Meituan (-3.3%), Kunlun Energy Co. (-3.1%), and China Tower Corp. (-2.7%).

data.title: Airbus rises after stable yearly deliveries data
data.description:
** Shares in Airbus rise 1.4% after the world's largest plane maker on Thursday reported 2024 deliveries roughly in line with market expectations and looked certain to maintain leadership of the jetmaking industry

** "A potential FY25 delivery guidance in line with our current assumption of 819 deliveries looks achievable," Deutsche Bank says

** Airbus' Q1 deliveries are likely to be weak due to few aircraft in inventory, but they will pick up in Q2 as the new LEAP HPT blades are built into new engines, alleviating engine shortages, Citi says

** Citi lifts its 2024 core earnings forecast for Airbus by 4% and raises its PT by 14% to 209 euros

** By 0811 GMT, the stock is up at 158.7 euros and among top performers on France's CAC 40 index, which is flat

($1 = 0.9713 euros)

data.title: SRF, Navin Fluorine slip upto 4% as investors book profits after recent surge
data.description:
Shares of specialty chemical players--SRF and Navin Fluorine slipped as much as 4 percent on January 10 as investors rushed to cash in profits off the counters after the recent surge in the stocks. While SRF zoomed nearly 14 percent, Navin Fluorine soared around 10 percent in the previous session, giving investors plenty headroom to book profits.

At 1.41 pm, shares of Navin Fluorine were trading with cuts of nearly 4 percent at Rs 3,689.80 on the NSE, while those of SRF were down around 3 percent at Rs 2,601.95. With today's fall, both the stocks also snapped their three-day winning streak.

The surge in the two stocks in the previous session were fueled by reports of an increase in prices of refrigerant gases by US gas distributors. IGas USA stated that the supply of R32 and R125 was been affected which resulted in an uptick in prices by up to 200 percent.

Follow our market blog to catch all the live action

The price hike for refrigerant gases bodes extremely well for the margins of specialty chemical makers like SRF and Navin Fluorine. Brokerage firm Equirus noted that a $1 per kilogram increase in realisations of the R32 refrigerant could boost SRF's EBITDA by Rs 260 crore. Similarly, Navin Fluorine's EBITDA could improve by Rs 77 crore for every $1 per kilogram rise in R32 gas realisations.

SRF has a production capacity of around 29,000 to 30,000 tonnes for R32, while Navin Fluorine's capacity stands at around 4,500 tonnes, with plans to double it by adding an additional 4,500 tonnes next month. Furthermore, SRF also has a capacity of around 7,000 tonnes for the R125 refrigerant.

The development also came as an unexpected boost for the two companies, especially since the chemical sector has faced earnings pressure over the past two years due to subdued global demand and an influx of Chinese inventory. As a result, the positive news was well-received by investors.Disclaimer: The views and investment tips expressed by experts on Moneycontrol are their own and not those of the website or its management. Moneycontrol advises users to check with certified experts before taking any investment decisions.

data.title: Market Chatter: Samsung's Harman International Unveils Advanced Automotive Intelligence at CES 2025
data.description:
Samsung Electronics' automotive and audio subsidiary Harman International unveiled its new "automotive intelligence" solutions at the CES 2025 on Wednesday, featuring personalized in-cabin experiences through AI and contextual awareness.

Key highlights comprise the "Luna" avatar, an AI-powered assistant integrated with Harman's Ready Vision QVUE AR head-up display, and "Ready Aware," which uses digital twins and machine learning to enhance situational awareness, The Korea Herald reported Thursday.

Shares of Samsung Electronics fell more than 1% at market close on Friday.

(Market Chatter news is derived from conversations with market professionals globally. This information is believed to be from reliable sources but may include rumor and speculation. Accuracy is not guaranteed.)

data.title: Shenwu Energy Saving's Subsidiary Signs 1 Billion Yuan Battery Plant Contract
data.description:
Shenwu Energy Saving's subsidiary, Jiangsu Metallurgical Design Institute, signed a 1 billion yuan contract with power transmission maintenance company Zhongqing Advanced Battery Manufacturing (Shihezi), according to a Shenzhen Stock Exchange disclosure on Thursday.

Jiangsu Metallurgical will construct a 6-gigawatt high-efficiency battery plant for Zhongqing Advanced Battery Manufacturing.

The project will have a land area of 94666.08 square meters and its construction is expected to start on Jan. 18 and end on March 31, 2026, the Chinese machine-made paper and adhesive manufacturer said.

The company's shares closed 9% lower.

data.title: Sterling slides for a fourth day amid bond market turmoil
data.description:
Sterling continued its slide on Friday, its fourth day of falls, as elevated global bond yields kept the currency under pressure.

The pound was down 0.17% against the dollar at $1.2286 , hovering close to Thursday's 14-month low of $1.2239.

Global borrowing costs have soared amid concerns about rising inflation, reduced chances of a drop in interest rates, and uncertainty over how U.S. President-elect Donald Trump will conduct foreign or economic policy.

This has propped up the dollar while sending ripples through other currencies and stocks.

Among the worst hit has been the UK market, with sterling having lost 1.9% since Tuesday.

The yield on British government bonds, or gilts, soared this week, driving the government's borrowing costs to their highest in over 16 years.

This is putting pressure on finance minister Rachel Reeves, potentially forcing her to cut future spending.

Benchmark 10-year gilt yields (GB10YT=RR) edged up in early trading on Friday to around 4.84%, but remained below Thursday's high of 4.925%, their highest since 2008.

But sterling continued to slide, as investor concern mounted about the country's finances.

"There remains clear concern over the likelihood that all of the Chancellor's fiscal headroom has now been eaten up by the sell-off in gilts, and the anaemic nature of UK economic growth," said Michael Brown, strategist at Pepperstone.

Traders are paying more to hedge against big swings in the pound than at any time since the March 2023 banking crisis.

One-month options volatility, a measure of demand for protection, hit a high of 10.9% on Thursday (GBP1MO=).

By Friday morning, this had retreated marginally to 10.08%.

Investors are now looking ahead to key U.S. jobs data published later in the session, to confirm their view that U.S. rates could stay higher for longer, which could cement the dollar's rally further.

data.title: France Household Spending Recovers in November
data.description:
Household consumption in France increased by 0.3% month-on-month in November 2024, rebounding from a downwardly revised 0.3% decline in October.

The recovery was primarily driven by higher consumption of engineered goods (+0.9% vs. -1.3% in October), durable goods (+1.9% vs. -0.4%), and other engineered goods (+0.2% vs. -0.1%).

In contrast, food consumption grew at a slower pace (0.3% vs. 1.3% in October), while energy consumption continued to decline (-0.8% vs. -1.2%), largely due to a further drop in electricity usage.

data.title: Spain Industrial Output Shrinks in November
data.description:
Industrial production in Spain fell by 0.4% year-on-year in November 2024, following a downwardly revised 1.5% increase in the prior month.

This marked the first decline in industrial activity after three consecutive months of growth, on lower production of capital goods (-2.8%) and energy (-1.9%).

On a seasonally adjusted monthly basis, industrial output shrank by 0.8% in November.

data.title: Alibaba May Report Accelerated 3Q Sales Growth — Market Talk
data.description: Alibaba could post accelerated growth in gross merchandise volume for fiscal 3Q compared with fiscal 2Q as its market share has continued to stabilize, HSBC analysts say in a note. Both China's trade-in program and the November shopping festival supported the company's accelerated GMV growth, the analysts say. Competition from ByteDance's Douyin and Tencent's WeChat should remain manageable, the analysts say, as they think Alibaba will remain committed to investing in user experience to defend market share. "Continual monetization improvement in China e-commerce coupled with recovery in earnings growth thereafter is crucial for a share price re-rating," the analysts say. HSBC projects the e-commerce giant's fiscal 3Q revenue to grow 8% on year. HSBC maintains a buy call on Allibaba with its ADR target price unchanged at $128.00. The ADRs last closed at $83.69. (sherry.qin@wsj.com)

data.title: Computer Age Management Services Surpasses 10 Million Policies in e-Insurance Accounts
data.description: Computer Age Management Services said it is managing over 10 million policies across 8 million e-Insurance Accounts, reflecting the growing shift by policyholders to digital platforms, according to a Thursday press release filed with the Indian bourses.

data.title: Singapore Central Bank Could Ease Monetary Policy in January — Market Talk
data.description: The continued cooling of Singapore's underlying inflation supports Nomura's view that the central bank could ease monetary policy settings later this month. The brokerage's FX strategy team. now assigns a 55% likelihood to the MAS easing its policy via a slight reduction in the slope of S$NEER appreciation, up from 40% previously. The MAS likely views current policy settings as restrictive given the risk posed by Trump's tariffs, say Euben Paracuelles and Charnon Boonnuch in a note. Nomura continues to expect 2025 average core inflation at 1.6% on year, near the lower end of MAS's forecast range of 1.5%-3.0%. However, it sees downside risks after a broad-based, sharper-than-expected decline in November from October. MAS is due to announce its policy decision no later than Jan. 31. (amanda.lee@wsj.com)

data.title: BEST Gets Non-Compliance Notice From NYSE
data.description:
BEST said Friday it received a notice from the New York Stock Exchange that it is not compliant with the exchange's listing rules due to its failure to timely file its Form 6-K for the half-year ended June 30, 2024.

The company said its shares will no longer be listed upon completion of its privatization efforts.

data.title: Sainsbury's Delivers Upbeat Quarter; Backed Guidance Seen as Reassuring — Market Talk
data.description: Sainsbury's 3Q update was positive, with sales growth against a challenging comparison point, Shore Capital analysts Clive Black and Darren Shirley write. The British grocer's unchanged expectations for retail adjusted operating profit are seen as reassuring, they say. Moreover, adjusted operating profit for its financial services division is now guided to be around 30 million pounds, leading to a 10 million pound upgrade to the U.K. investment group's pretax profit estimate. The Argos business held its own, with a 1.4% drop in sales amid a tough market but growth in the Christmas period, the analysts say. Sainsbury's looks particularly attractive given significant assets and cash generation, together with market share gains and a dividend yield of 5.2%, they say. Shares are down 2.0% at 25.78 pounds. (michael.susin@wsj.com)

data.title: News Highlights: Top Global Markets News of the Day - Friday at 3 AM ET
data.description:
U.K. Pound, Bonds Slide; U.S. Stock Market Closed

Volatility in U.K. asset prices evokes memories of the 2022 market meltdown.

China's Central Bank Suspends Purchase of Treasury Bonds in Open Markets

The move comes as long-term yields hit fresh lows amid expectations of more monetary policy easing.

Investors are on edge as Friday's jobs report could make or break the stock-market rally

Rising Treasury yields are ratcheting up the anxiety for equity investors. Thursday's stock-market closure offered only a brief reprieve from the mounting tension.

The World's Bond Markets Are Uneasy. It's the U.S.'s Fault.

The yield on 10-year U.K. government debt rose to the highest since 2008, the latest casualty of a global rout spurred by rising yields on Treasury notes.

The Stock Market Is Looking for Its Next Boost. Earnings Won't Provide.

Companies across the board are less likely to surpass expectations, while stocks have already taken the past few years to reflect growing earnings and a growing economy.

L.A. wildfires have caused more than $135 billion in economic losses - and counting

Fires across Southern California are already one of the most damaging in the states history.

U.S. Mortgage Rates Climb to Highest Since July

U.S. mortgage rates are back at their highest level since last summer, frustrating home buyers waiting for a break from some of the steepest borrowing costs in more than a decade.

Why two Fed officials doubt the need for many more rate cuts

One official warned that "progress on inflation could continue to stall."

Bank of England Monitoring Gilt Market, But Sees Moves as Orderly, Says Breeden

"So far the moves have been orderly," Breeden said after a speech in Scotland. "So far so good."

U.N. Raises Global Growth Forecast, but Warns of Potential Inflation Risks

An increase in trade barriers would likely reignite price pressures, the organization says ahead of Trump's plan to impose new tariffs.

data.title: News Highlights: Top Company News of the Day - Friday at 3 AM ET
data.description:
TikTok Is Heading to the Supreme Court. How Meta and Snap Could Benefit from a Ban.

The Supreme Court will hear oral arguments from TikTok on Friday.

The Pacific Palisades Inferno Won't Engulf the Insurance Industry

The insurance industry has been thriving as climate change increases the demand for coverage.

Fox Must Face Smartmatic's Defamation Lawsuit, Appeals Court Rules

The voting-machine company seeks billions in damages.

BlackRock Withdraws From Climate Coalition, Backpedaling Again on ESG

The world's largest asset manager is latest Wall Street firm to quit climate groups.

Elon Musk's xAI Launches Stand-Alone Grok App

The AI startup launched its first stand-alone consumer app, as the company tries to catch up with more established players such as OpenAI and Google in the generative AI race.

Wells Fargo Taps JPMorgan Executive to Lead Its Credit-Card Business

Growth in credit cards has been a focus for CEO Charlie Scharf.

Delta Air Lines' Earnings Can Make or Break the Sector's Rally. 1 Metric Is Key.

Airline stocks have been flying recently, which makes earnings season tricky to navigate for investors.

Southwest Airlines CFO Tammy Romo to Retire as Airline Embarks on Turnaround

Romo is retiring after more than three decades with the carrier as it works on a turnaround after lagging behind some of its peers in recent years.

DHL Buys Into the Growing Retail Returns Business

The acquisition of a returns services provider comes as retailers are grappling with the higher volumes that shoppers are sending back.

KKR, PSP Investments Partner to Buy Stake in Two American Electric Power Companies

KKR is partnering with the Public Sector Pension Investment Board to buy a 19.9% interest in two units of American Electric Power for $2.82 billion.

data.title: Update: Market Chatter: Azul, Gol Parent Expected to Sign Memorandum of Understanding for Potential Deal
data.description:
(Updates with Gol's response in the final paragraph.)

Azul and Abra, the parent company of Gol, are expected to sign a memorandum of understanding for the potential merger of the two Brazilian airlines, news outlet Valor Economico reported Wednesday.

The objective of the memorandum is to discuss details that could pertain to the deal, the report said.

Azul and Gol declined to comment when contacted by MT Newswires.

(Market Chatter news is derived from conversations with market professionals globally. This information is believed to be from reliable sources but may include rumor and speculation. Accuracy is not guaranteed.)

data.title: PTA-News: ADX Energy Ltd.: Variation of Loan Note Agreements
data.description:
ADX Energy Ltd.

by Vesna Blazevic

Business news for the stock market Wien (pta/10.01.2025/09:06) - "Extension to March 2026 provides financial flexibility to pursue multiple, immediate growth opportunities" Key Points: * The revised terms apply to twenty five (25) Loan Notes of A$50,000 each (A$1.25 million in total) issued in July 2023. Five (5) Loan Notes of A$50,000 each (A$250,000 in total) will be repaid on the original repayment date of 11 January 2025. * The repayment date of the remaining A$1.25 million principal is31 March 2026 with loan interest rate continuing to accrue. Existing options previously issued in relation to the Loan Notes will lapse on 11 January 2025. New options will be granted for Loan Note holders who have executed variation agreements. * The agreement by the Loan Note holders to extend repayment provides ADX with the financial flexibility to pursue multiple identified growth opportunities and reposition the Company for further drilling and development activities before requiring access to further capital. * ADX will immediately focus on the following near term value development and strategic growth opportunities: * further evaluation and testing of the Welchau-1 well, * further oil appraisal and development activities in Upper Austria, * pursuing a multi target - low risk, shallow gas exploration play in Upper Austria, * ongoing portfolio development in Upper Austria, * the potential licencing of its Sicily Channel exploration permit offshore Italy, and * an ongoing partnership formation program to fund ongoing activities. For more details please see the PDF file. data.title: SPX: S&P 500 Futures Fall Ahead of Jobs Data. Earnings Season Is Around the Corner
data.description:
Key points:
  • S&P 500 futures drop ahead of bell
  • Jobs data to spark broad volatility
  • Earnings season cranks up next week

December jobs are expected to come in at 164,000. Earnings season is next week and banks will kick it off.

  • S&P 500 futures were looking a bit lousy Friday morning as investors were getting ready to come back to work after Thursday was a day off in honor of the passing of the 39th US President Jimmy Carter. There isn’t much time to scramble around as today’s economic calendar brings a top-of-the-list report. The nonfarm payrolls, or simply jobs data, will show how many people found jobs in December.
  • Analysts have pinned hopes on the figure of 154,000 new hires, a marked downgrade from November’s 227,000. Still, some speculate that this figure might be on the low end due to the month’s holidays and potential extra effort by employers to recruit seasonal workers. In case of a surprise readout — a figure that’s too low or too high above the consensus view — stocks might get rattled due to elevated volatility.
  • Looking ahead to next week, it’s the start of the earnings season and — as is tradition — Wall Street’s top banks will be the ones to crank it up. Bellwethers of consumer and business sentiment JPMorgan , Wells Fargo , and Goldman Sachs all provide their December-quarter updates on January 15. UnitedHealth , the infamous health insurer, will be another highlight of the week.

data.title: Rheinmetall: Blackned Specialises in Software for the Digitalisation of Armed Forces

European indices hold a more tentative mood to start the session
data.description:
Key points:
  • Eurostoxx -0.1%
  • Germany DAX flat
  • France CAC 40 flat
  • UK FTSE -0.2%
  • Spain IBEX -0.6%

Meanwhile, US futures are keeping more cautious with S&P 500 futures seen down 0.3% currently. That is setting up for a more tentative and slightly cautious mood overall as the focus turns to the non-farm payrolls data later. That's the only game in town left for trading this week.

data.title: Rheinmetall Did Not Provide Financial Details of the Share Acquisition
China has ample fiscal space to bolster growth this year
data.description:
China has ample fiscal policy space and tools to support economic growth this year and it will step up spending to spur investment, Vice Finance Minister Liao Min said on Friday.

More proactive fiscal policy can be expected in 2025, in terms of its strength, efficiency and timing, Liao said at a press conference.

"Facing new situations and problems in both internal and external environments, we have sufficient policy space and tools," Liao said.

The authorities will closely monitor the international and domestic situation and adjust policies to provide strong support for economic and social development.

In December, China's top leaders pledged to increase the budget deficit, issue more debt and loosen monetary policy to maintain a stable economic growth rate, according to a summary of the Central Economic Work Conference.

Reuters has reported that Beijing would target a budget deficit of 4% of gross domestic product this year, while maintaining an economic growth target of around 5%.

In 2024, local governments issued a total of 4 trillion yuan ($545.5 billion) in special bonds, including 3.9 trillion yuan under the annual quota and 100 billion yuan carried over from 2023, Liao said.

In addition, local governments issued 2 trillion yuan in special bonds to swap for hidden local debt by Dec. 18, he added.

($1 = 7.3326 Chinese yuan)

data.title: Serco Group Target Cut to 175p From 225p by Jefferies
Serco Group Cut to Hold From Buy by Jefferies
Bank Indonesia's Rupiah Priority May Limit Easing Cycle — Market Talk
data.description: Bank Indonesia's strong emphasis on foreign-exchange stability will likely make it difficult to deliver additional rate cuts in the coming months, Nomura analysts say. That's particularly true given that President-elect Trump is threatening to raise tariffs on imports and the Fed is turning less dovish, Euben Paracuelles and Nabila Amani say in a note. The economists pencil in one 25bp cut in 4Q 2025. The Indonesian central bank meets next week. They see Indonesia's GDP growth slowing slightly from an estimated 5.0% expansion in 2024. Household spending will likely be held back by still-weak consumer sentiment in 1H before edging up in 2H as public-sector spending improves. (fabiana.negrinochoa@wsj.com)

data.title: Hang Seng Tech Index Closes 1.2% Lower at 4260.82
